---
title: "Shell commands"
---

## Shell commands

If you haven't used a shell before to access a computer, it can be intimidating!  But knowing just a few commands can get you quite far.

Also known as bash, unix, or linux commands.

## Navigate

- `cd`: change directory
- `ls`: list 
- `pwd`: print working directory

## File management

- `mkdir`: make a directory
- `rmdir`: remove a directory
- `cp`: copy files
- `mv`: move files
- `rm`: remove files

## Recursion

- Adding `-r` makes the command recursive, which means it will apply to all files in a directory, not just one at a time.
- Make sure you know what you are doing! `rm -r` deletes a whole directory!

## Seeing and editing files

- `cat` prints out the contents of a file.
- `nano` is a simple text editor that allows you to edit files on the terminal. There are other options, but I find this is the most user-friendly because it has instructions at the bottom. Running `nano filename` will start the nano editor.
